Empires & Puzzles Cookie Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Hero Strategy Guide

Cookie brings a mix of immediate mana reduction and delayed devastation to Empires & Puzzles. Is his Mystery Potion a recipe for success or a half-baked mechanic? We analyze his stats, synergy potential, and whether the three-turn wait is worth the payoff in the current fast-paced meta.
Cookie Review: The Gnome Scientist's Delayed Explosions
Cookie: The Gnome Scientist Who Plays With Fire (and Timers)

Cookie arrived in December 2023 as part of the Santa's Challenge Winter event, introducing himself as a Legendary Fire Rogue with a penchant for chemistry and chaos. Unlike the jolly gift-givers of the past, Cookie is here to take things away specifically your mana. He operates as a disruptor, combining immediate mana cutting with a delayed "time bomb" mechanic that blocks mana generation entirely if it detonates. He represents a shift towards more complex, timer-based ailments in the game, demanding patience and precise timing from his users.

Visually, Cookie is a delightful twist on the Christmas elf trope. He isn't making toys; he is mixing volatile compounds. The art depicts a weathered, slightly maniacal gnome scientist clad in red, surrounded by bubbling beakers and flasks. The lighting is excellent, highlighting the mischievous glint in his eye. It is a fantastic character design that steps away from the generic warrior aesthetic, giving us a character who looks like he might accidentally blow up the workshop rather than defend it.

Cookie boasts a very modern stat distribution that leans heavily towards survivability. With 968 Defense and 1666 Health in his base form, he is built like a tank. His Attack stat of 916 is respectable but not earth-shattering, which aligns with his role as a support and disruption hero rather than a primary damage dealer. When compared to the latest releases, his stats hold up well, ensuring he can survive long enough to fire his Average speed special, which is critical for his delayed mechanics to work.
The "Mystery Potions" skill is a multi-layered ability that requires a nuanced understanding of tempo.
The 20% mana cut to all enemies is the bread and butter here. At Average speed, this is a solid disruption tool that buys your team time.
Summoning minions with 10% HP and Attack for all allies adds a layer of meat shields, which is always welcome, especially given the Winter family bonus interactions.

The "Mystery Potion" ailment is the controversial centerpiece. It sits on enemies for three turns. If it isn't cleansed, it deals 450 damage and, more importantly, stops mana generation for three turns.

The primary criticism here is the delay. Three turns is an eternity in modern Empires & Puzzles. However, the threat alone forces the opponent to act. They must cleanse or block it, often burning resources they wanted to save for offense. If they cannot cleanse it, the mana stop is devastating.
Average. He needs 10 tiles to fire. With a level 23 Mana Troop (or level 11 Magic/Styx/Cyclops), this drops to 9 tiles, which is the sweet spot for 3-matches.

The 450 damage is fixed and does not scale with attack buffs, but it ignores defense to an extent (it's a status effect damage). Across 5 enemies, that is a potential 2250 total damage, but it is rarely realized on all targets due to cleanses.
A 10% HP minion on a hero with ~1800 HP (emblemed) is roughly 180 HP buffer per ally. Across a team of 5, that is 900 HP of "overhealing."
20% flat reduction. This is often enough to drop a charged enemy from "Full" back down to "Waiting," potentially preventing a team wipe.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Winter family bonus is minion-centric, boosting the health and attack of minions. Since Cookie summons minions for everyone, this synergy is built-in. While minions have hard counters (like Grimble, Ogima, or Alucard), they remain annoying buffers that absorb sniper fire.
Cookie does not have a specific passive skill, which is a slight drawback compared to newer event heroes who often come with innate resistances or mana boosts.

is one of the strongest Aether Powers in the game. For the first 6 turns, Cookie reflects all status ailments back to the caster. This makes him incredibly annoying to shut down with traditional controllers like Proteus or Hel at the start of a match. It guarantees he will likely get his first special off uninterrupted.

Upon release, Cookie was met with mixed reviews. The community compared him unfavorably to Carol, another hero with a delayed bomb mechanic that was considered underwhelming. Players felt the 3-turn wait was too long for the payoff.

His stats are still high, keeping him relevant as a meat shield. However, the mechanics of "delayed damage" have struggled to keep up with the "immediate devastation" meta of heroes who deal 400% damage to all instantly.

Cookie is a viable but niche hero. He is not a plug-and-play superstar for every team. He shines in specific formats like Rush Attack wars (where his speed becomes Very Fast) or Bloody Battle tournaments (where his minions provide pseudo-healing and his ailments add pressure). In standard raids, he requires a team built around stalling to be effective.
maximizes his primary role as a disruptor and tank/flank. He needs to stay alive to keep cutting mana and refreshing potion timers.
His minions inherit stats from him, so lower attack means slightly weaker minion hits, but minion damage is negligible anyway.
Increases the survivability of his minions slightly (since minion HP is based on caster HP, but minion Attack is based on caster Attack).
The 450 damage from the potion is fixed and does not increase with his Attack stat. Embleming for Attack yields diminishing returns.
path is recommended. Cookie's job is to annoy and disrupt, not to snipe. Keep him alive, and he keeps the enemy mana bars empty.
A 20% cut to all enemies is always useful, regardless of the meta.
His Aether Power makes him immune to early-game shutdowns.
Great base stats make him a tough nut to crack.
The threat of the potion forces enemies to use their cleanse skills defensively rather than optimally.
Waiting 3 turns for the damage and mana block is agonizingly slow in a game where matches are decided in 4 turns.
A simple cleanse completely negates the "Mystery Potion" part of his skill.
Ailment immunity (Monks, Clerics, Garnet, Vanda) stops the potion from landing entirely.
The damage cap of 450 does not scale, making it less threatening as hero HP pools continue to grow.
At Very Fast speed, he can fire repeatedly. Note that refreshing the ailment does
speed up the timer, but the immediate 20% mana cut becomes oppressive when fired every other turn.
Minions provide "health" without healing, and his Rogue class gives him a chance to dodge.
In high-level tower stages, his mana control can prevent bosses from firing their one-shot kill moves.
This team is designed to never let the enemy fire. Cookie cuts mana, Waterpipe's minions reduce mana, and Luna controls mana generation. The enemy dies of frustration.
Place Cookie next to Ludwig. Ludwig charges Cookie repeatedly. While the potion timers don't stack, the constant 20% mana cuts will paralyze the enemy team while you wait for the bombs to go off.
(Hathor, Aramis, Garnet). These heroes make Cookie's potion completely useless.
Other "Bomb" heroes like Carol. Since you can usually only have one instance of a specific status effect (depending on ID), they don't really "combo" well; they just do similar slow things.

If you pulled Cookie early, he is a great asset. His stats alone will carry you through map stages, and the minions will keep your weaker heroes alive. Don't worry about the 3-turn delay; in lower-level play, matches last longer, so the bombs will go off more frequently.

Cookie is a luxury toy. He is fun for diversity in wars but is unlikely to crack your top defense team unless you lack the premier mana controllers. He is worth taking to 3/70 to test, but save the Rings and Alpha Aethers unless you have a specific Rush War team that needs a red mana controller.
The community reaction to Cookie was essentially a collective shrug followed by heated debate.
Many players immediately labeled him "Useless trash" due to the 3-turn delay. One user noted, "If it takes 3 turns to deal 450 damage, you are better off using almost any sniper."
Others found value in his disruption. "He isn't a destroyer... but he chokes the mana out of your team."
There was plenty of salt from players who pulled him instead of the coveted Ginger. "I pulled him twice... That's how I know he is bad."

Post-release testing showed that while he isn't "OP," the combination of high stats and mana cut makes him a surprisingly sturdy flank in specific setups, specifically when paired with heavy hitters who need time to charge.

Cookie is the definition of a "thinking player's hero." He doesn't offer instant gratification. You can't just fire him and watch the enemy team evaporate. You have to strategize, stall, and control the board to let his chemistry set do the work. While he might not be the flavor of the month, a well-timed explosion combined with a mana block can checkmate an opponent who thought they were safe. He is not a top-tier god, but he is certainly a tasty treat for those who enjoy control archetypes.
A solid B+ hero who excels in Rush and stall teams but crumbles against heavy cleanse metas. Keep him in the jar for the right occasion.

A comprehensive review of Cookie, the Legendary Fire Rogue from the Winter family in Empires & Puzzles. Analyze his mana control mechanics, delayed damage potential, and determine if this Gnome Scientist belongs on your team.
